item.title: New paper on Forgetting in Deep Graph Networks
item.description:
Our paper on Forgetting in Deep Graph Network is out on arXiv.
The paper has been accepted at the workshop on Graph Learning Benchmarks, within the 2021 Web Conference (WWW).

item.title: New paper on Forgetting in Deep Graph Network
item.description:
Our paper Catastrophic Forgetting in Deep Graph Networks: an Introductory Benchmark for Graph Classification has been accepted at the WWW Workshop on Graph Learning Benchmarks (GLB 2021).
Really excited about this paper, which is one of the first contributions studying graph networks and continual learning! Joint work together with Federico Errica, Antonio Carta and Davide Bacciu 🎉

item.title: Avalanche: an End-to-End library for Continual Learning
item.description:
Avalanche is a Continual Learning library based on PyTorch.
The library has been developed within ContinualAI and has now been released to the public.
You can check out the documentation here!

item.title: Talk on Evaluation Protocol and Metrics in CL
item.description:
I gave a brief talk at the last ContinualAI meetup on Evaluation Protocols and metrics for Continual Learning.
Here are my slides!

item.title: Neural Networks for high-school students
item.description:
I got the opportunity and the pleasure to talk about Neural Networks to students of high-school Liceo Gobetti, Florence.
These students are starting their journey towards a more detailed understanding of Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning and all the accomplishments and problems these topics bring into play.

Thanks to the Italian Association for Artificial Intelligence for inviting me to this event!

item.title: Teaching Machine Learning at Tree
item.description: I will be teaching the Machine Learning module within Data Science (free) course offered by Tree. In the course, we will focus on supervised and unsupervised learning paradigms, with applications to Computer Vision scenarios.

item.title: Chief Scientist of ContinualAI
item.description:
I am proud to join ContinualAI research as Chief Scientist.
In addition to the (awesome) CLAI wiki, I will help in managing key projects within the community.

item.title: EEML summer school
item.description: I will participate in the Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School next July. Even if virtually held, I am sure it will be a stimulating experience, with many important speakers already announced. I will present a poster of my recent work on Continual Learning with Gated Incremental Memories. Hope to see many CL researchers there!

item.title: ContinualAI wiki
item.description:
I am now the Principal Maintainer of ContinualAI wiki platform. In there, you can find information about the exciting world of Continual Learning and a list of CL papers which should be very easy for you to browse.
We are working to keep the list updated, but we can really use your help. Feel free to contribute to this open-source project by taking a look at our GitHub wiki repository, which includes a README with all the (very simple) details.
You can find more information about ContinualAI by visiting the official website. In case you want to get in touch or get involved in the community, the first thing to do is joining the Slack, where a growing number of CL researchers is finding a great place to cooperate and discuss.

item.title: Pre-print of Gated Incremental Memories for Continual Learning is out on arXiv
item.description:
Our paper about Continual Learning with Gated Incremental Memories, a progressive recurrent neural network, is available as a pre-print on arXiv!.
It has also been accepted at IJCNN 2020.

item.title: Continual Learning @ IJCNN 2020
item.description:
Our new paper Continual Learning with Gated Incremental Memories for Sequential Data Processing has been accepted for presentation at IJCNN 2020, in the context of the World Congress on Computational Intelligence.
Gated Incremental Memory is a progressive Recurrent Neural Network which is able to learn multiple incoming tasks while at the same time mitigating forgetting of previous knowledge.
It is a joint work together with Davide and Antonio. Yay 😃

item.title: Il Dilemma dell'Algoritmo - PMI Northern Italy Chapter
item.description:
Me and Paolo Santinello, from KlinK, presented a talk at the annual meeting of the Project Management Institute - Northern Italy Chapter. Our talk Il Dilemma dell’Algoritmo - questioni etiche e strumenti di IA nei processi decisionali di Project Management was given in front of more than 300 PMI Project Managers and partners.
We talked about how Agent Based Modeling can deal with uncertainty and ethics in the context of a specific project, raising the question of accountability of a decision in the presence of multiple options.

item.title: Smart Applications Teaching Assistant
item.description:
The Smart Applications course, taught by Prof. Vincenzo Gervasi, is approaching the end. I had a lot of fun in assisting both projects and really hope to see them in action soon 😄

Congrats to all students of Art and Sport projects!

item.title: Intel Student Ambassador for AI
item.description: I have been chosen to represent University of Pisa in Intel® Student Ambassador for AI program.
